expectations.text: Response uses resource:action permission pattern (e.g., booking:read)
expectations.evidence: Permissions use 'resource:action' pattern: 'bookings:read', 'bookings:create', 'rooms:update', 'billing:delete', etc. (Note: uses plural 'bookings' instead of singular 'booking' but the pattern is correct.)

expectations.text: Response defines role-permission mappings following least-privilege
expectations.evidence: Three roles with progressively restricted permissions: admin gets all 16 permissions, staff gets 7 (booking CRUD + read-only), member gets 3 (bookings:read, bookings:create, rooms:read). Follows least-privilege principle.

expectations.text: Response includes Drizzle pgTable schemas for roles and permissions
expectations.evidence: Section 3 defines: "export const roles = pgTable('roles', {...})" and "export const permissions = pgTable('permissions', {...})" using Drizzle pgTable.
Both junction tables defined: "export const rolePermissions = pgTable('role_permissions', {...})" and "export const userRoles = pgTable('user_roles', {...})" with composite primary keys.

expectations.text: Response includes foreign keys and indexes
expectations.evidence: Foreign keys are present (".references(() => roles.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' })"), but no indexes are defined on any table. The with_skill version included indexes on userId, roleId, permissionId columns, but this version has none.
